source_metadata.title_raw: Clementine Bonner Klein - A-19-181 - October 10, 2019 - Monterey

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 10, 2019

Clementine Bonner Klein
Acting City Clerk, City of Monterey
580 Pacific St, Rm 6
Monterey, CA 93940

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-19-181

Dear Ms. Klein:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

QUESTION

May a local filing officer accept electronic filings of the Form 501 Statement of Intention ("Statement of Intention")? If so, in which manner should the electronic filing be accepted?

CONCLUSION

Yes. A local filing officer may accept electronic filings of the Statement of Intention, so long as the candidate files it with the same filing officer with whom they would file their original campaign statement. If the filing officer has an active e-filing vendor used for campaign statements, this system may also be used for e-filing the Statement of Intention.

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

You are the acting city clerk for the City of Monterey. You have requested advice regarding the filing procedure of the Statement of Intention and would like to know whether the local filing officer may accept electronic filings of the Statement of Intention. You have stated that the Statement of Intention instructions indicate "electronic filing may be required," which seems to

_________________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-19-181
Page No. 2

indicate to you that electronic filing is possible. It seems that the approved vendors for e-filing
campaign forms do not currently offer electronic filing of the Statement of Intention because it is
not included in the specifications for electronically filing forms with the California Secretary of
State ("Secretary of State.") However, the Secretary of State has informed you that their
specifications do not include the Statement of Intention, only because there is no Secretary of State
involvement in the Statement of Intention at the local level, as its filing is purely local and
completed prior to any committees being formed or any funds being raised/spent. On September 11,
2019, you provided additional facts stating the City of Monterey has an active contract with the e-
filing vendor Netfile, which is already in use for the Statement of Economic Interest Form 700 and
is poised to be used for campaign statement e-filing following the second reading of an ordinance
that was scheduled to occur on September 17, 2019.

ANALYSIS

Section 85200 provides:

"Prior to the solicitation or receipt of any contribution or loan, an individual who
intends to be a candidate for an elective state office, as that term is defined by Section
82024, shall file with the Secretary of State an original statement, signed under penalty of
perjury, of intention to be a candidate for a specific office.

"An individual who intends to be a candidate for any other elective office shall file
the statement of intention with the same filing officer and in the same location as the
individual would file an original campaign statement pursuant to subdivisions (b), (c), and
(d) of Section 84215.

With regard to campaign reports and statements, Section 84215(d) provides:

"City elected officers, candidates for city office, their controlled committees,
committees formed or existing primarily to support or oppose candidates or local measures
to be voted upon in one city, and city general purpose committees shall file the original and
one copy with the clerk of the city and are not required to file with the local elections
official of the county in which they are domiciled."

Pursuant to Section 85200, if a local candidate is required to file their original campaign
statement with the City of Monterey, that candidate shall also file their Statement of Intention with
the City of Monterey. Section 84215(d) clarifies that city elected officials are to file with the city
clerk, thus candidates for city elective office for the City of Monterey must file their Statement of
Intention with the City of Monterey.

Section 84615 of the Act permits cities and counties to mandate electronic filing of
campaign reports for candidates and committees in their jurisdiction. Under Section 84615(a), an
ordinance must be adopted by the local legislative body approving the use of electronic filing. The
electronic filing system must be able to accept filings in a format that is compatible with the
Secretary of State's electronic filing system, it must ensure the integrity of the data transmitted, it
must issue to filers a confirmation that reports were received and meet other requirements. (Section

File No. A-19-181
Page No. 3

84615.) The system must permit filers to meet the requirement for signing statements under penalty of perjury and must permit filers to complete and submit filings free of charge. (Section 84615.)

Although Section 84615 is not controlling for the Statement of Intention, as long as the electronic filing system contains a procedure for filers to comply with the requirements that they sign statements and reports under penalty of perjury as required under Section 81004, and as required by Section 85200 for statements of intention, you would not have to request the original signature be separately filed on paper. Accordingly, The Statement of Intention may be filed in the same manner and with the same filing officer as the candidate's campaign statements.

Based on additional facts provided to our office on September 11, 2019, the City of Monterey has an active contract with Netfile, which is already in use for the Statement of Economic Interest Form 700 and is poised to be used for campaign statements following the second reading of an ordinance that was scheduled to occur on September 17, 2019. Accordingly, the City of Monterey may also accept e-filed Statement of Intentions for local candidates running for city elective office through Netfile, as Netfile is an approved vendor that permits filers to comply with the requirements that they sign statement and reports under penalty of perjury.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Katelyn Greene
Counsel, Legal Division

KG:aja
